medical_history.chief_complaint: Progressive erythematous nodules localized on the back, lomber region, the left gluteus and the right femoral lateral region
medical_history.history_of_present_illness: 2-year history of progressive erythematous nodules, no fever, weight loss or night sweats, no enlargement of the regional lymph nodes, no hepatosplenomegaly, normal blood counts and morphology, elevated ALT level (67 U/l), positivity of HBs Ag, increased HBV DNA level (19,432 copy/ml with 3470 UI/ml), normal liver USG

physical_examination.general_examination: No fever, weight loss or night sweats, no enlargement of the regional lymph nodes, no hepatosplenomegaly
physical_examination.specialty_examination: Histopathological examination of skin biopsy showed lymphoid proliferation in coarse centroblastic cells at the intermediate consisting of centrocytical cells with mediate magnitude forming infiltration with nodular style at dermis and subcutaneous fatty tissue. Immunohistochemical staining revealed positivity for CD 20, CD 45 RO, CD 23, whereas CD 10, cycline D1, BCL 6 were negative. Scattered CD 3 and CD 5 positivity were also observed in lymphocytic infiltrate. κ-Light chain and λ-light chain were scantly positive in plasma cells.

laboratory_examinations.findings: Within normal limits, except for an elevated ALT level (67 U/l)

laboratory_examinations.changes_over_time: Returned to normal limits (<2000 copy/ml with <357 UI/ml) after treatment

laboratory_examinations.findings: Elevated ALT level (67 U/l), later normalized after treatment

other_tests.findings: Lymphoid proliferation in coarse centroblastic cells at the intermediate consisting of centrocytical cells with mediate magnitude forming infiltration with nodular style at dermis and subcutaneous fatty tissue. Immunohistochemical staining revealed positivity for CD 20, CD 45 RO, CD 23, whereas CD 10, cycline D1, BCL 6 were negative. Scattered CD 3 and CD 5 positivity were also observed in lymphocytic infiltrate. κ-Light chain and λ-light chain were scantly positive in plasma cells.

diagnosis.final_diagnosis: Primary cutaneous marginal zone B-cell lymphoma (PCMZL)
diagnosis.diagnostic_reasoning: The diagnosis of PCMZL was made based on the clinical presentation of progressive erythematous nodules, immunohistological findings (positivity for CD 20, CD 45 RO, CD 23, and negativity for CD 10, cycline D1, BCL 6), and the absence of extracutaneous involvement by extensive systemic workup.

treatment_information.dosage_or_details: 375 mg/m2 once a week as an intravenous infusion in 1 l of normal saline for 8 weeks
